story.iWant: a ProjectService layer that handles CRUD operations, member management, and API coordination with the Moku API
story.soThat: the desktop application can manage project collaboration features with proper caching, retry logic, and permission enforcement
story.tasks: - Implement ProjectService Core (CRUD operations: create, list, get, update, delete) - Implement Member Management (getMembers, addMember, removeMember, updateMemberRole) - Implement Permission System (hasPermission with role permission matrix) - Implement API Retry Logic (max 3 attempts, exponential backoff) - Integration and Testing (unit tests, permission matrix, retry logic, E2E CRUD flow)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: ProjectService.create() successfully creates project via Moku API and caches result
ProjectService.list() returns user's owned + shared projects
ProjectService.get() retrieves project from cache (if fresh) or API (if stale)
ProjectService.update() modifies project and invalidates cache
ProjectService.delete() removes project and all associated cache entries
ProjectService.getMembers() returns member list from cache or API
ProjectService.addMember() adds member and invalidates members cache
ProjectService.removeMember() removes member with role validation (must be owner)
ProjectService.hasPermission() correctly evaluates user role against permission matrix
All API calls include retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff)

doc.section: ProjectService: CRUD operations, member management, invitations, API coordination - Inputs: Project data, member operations, invitation requests - Outputs: Project entities, operation results

Moku API Endpoints: - POST /api/projects - Create project - GET /api/projects - List user's projects - GET /api/projects/{id} - Get project details - PATCH /api/projects/{id} - Update project - DELETE /api/projects/{id} - Delete project (CASCADE) - GET /api/projects/{id}/members - List members - POST /api/projects/{id}/members - Add member - DELETE /api/projects/{id}/members/{userId} - Remove member - PATCH /api/projects/{id}/members/{userId} - Update member role - POST /api/projects/{id}/upgrade-to-shared - Upgrade personal to shared

Performance: - Project list retrieval: <300ms (cache hit), <1s (cache miss / API call) - Project creation: <2s end-to-end - Member list retrieval: <200ms (cached), <800ms (API call) - Cache lookup: <10ms
Security: - Role-based permissions enforced at desktop layer - Defense in depth: Moku API also enforces permissions - JWT tokens passed with all API requests
Reliability: - Retry logic for API calls: max 3 attempts, exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) - Graceful degradation: if Moku API unavailable, show cached data with staleness warning

code.file: Existing ProjectService implementation pattern: - Singleton pattern with getInstance() - Event listeners for project created/updated/deleted - wrapElectronCall for error handling - Methods: loadProjects, createProject, updateProject, deleteProject, getThreadCount
Project store pattern: - Svelte writable store - Methods: setProjects, addProject, updateProject, deleteProject - Sorting by updatedAt

Existing Project type: { id, title, description, createdAt, updatedAt, deletedAt, metadata, privacyMode } NEW Project type will add: { type: 'personal'|'shared', color, icon, memberCount, threadCount }
IPC handler pattern: - ipcMain.handle for each operation - toRendererProject conversion function - broadcast function for events - Performance logging with logPerformance - Authentication checks

dependencies.dependency: HTTP client for Moku API calls

constraints.constraint: All project data stored on Moku API (cloud-first for collaboration)
Desktop maintains read-through cache with 5-minute TTL
RBAC enforced at both API and desktop layers (defense in depth)
API calls must include retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
Permission checks before every mutating operation
BLOCKER: E1-S4 (Project API Implementation) must be complete

interface.name: IProjectService
interface.method: Creates project via Moku API and caches result
Returns user's owned + shared projects from cache or API
Retrieves project from cache (if fresh) or API (if stale)
Deletes project and all associated cache entries
Returns member list from cache or API

method.name: addMember
method.signature: addMember(projectId: string, userId: string, role: ProjectMemberRole): Promise<ProjectMember>

interface.method: Adds member and invalidates members cache
Removes member with role validation (must be owner)
Evaluates user role against permission matrix

tests.standards: - Unit tests: 85%+ coverage target - Mock Moku API for unit tests - Test all permission matrix combinations (3 roles × 10 permissions = 30 test cases) - Integration tests with real cache - E2E tests for full CRUD flow - Performance benchmarks: API latency P50/P95/P99

ideas.idea: Test project creation via Moku API, verify cached result with 5-minute TTL
Test list() returns mix of owned and shared projects
Test cache hit scenario (fresh data) vs cache miss (expired data)
Test update invalidates cache, next get() fetches from API
Test delete removes project and cascades to cache (members, threads)
Test getMembers with cache hit/miss scenarios
Test addMember invalidates cache, next getMembers fetches fresh
Test removeMember: owner succeeds, non-owner fails, last owner prevented
Test hasPermission: owner can invite, editor cannot, viewer cannot delete
Test retry logic: simulate API failures, verify exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s)
